summary: A legendary Providence show with smoking Bertha, Eyes and Morning Dew; audience recording by Jim Wise is clear enough despite first‑set sound trouble.
review: Fans hail this 1983 Providence concert as one of the era’s highlights – the second set erupts with a monster Bertha, a soaring Eyes jam and a heartfelt Morning Dew, while the first set closes powerfully on LLR→China Cat. The audience recording captured by Jim Wise is generally good, though listeners note some sound‑trouble in the opening set.

ai_rating.rationale: Four out of five reviewers gave 5 stars and praised multiple standout songs; only one moderate 3‑star review slightly lowers the average. Recording quality is deemed good, so overall rating is high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear audience capture with solid ambience; first‑set suffers from occasional sound trouble/muddiness but overall listenable and praised for Jim Wise's effort.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set builds to a thrilling LLR→China Cat finish; second set opens unusually with Esau and then explodes into high‑energy jams (Bertha, Eyes, Throwing Stones) delivering an unforgettable night.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for incredible solos in Eyes and strong riffs throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Phil: Described as blowing the roof off with his bass on Morning Dew.
band_member_comments.Bob: Great vocals all night; notable intro to "Don't Ease Me In".
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent (keyboard) noted for nice riff work during jams.
